title: Flip-Top Storage Bench with Cubbies
summary: A plywood storage box with a hinged lid and an open front cubby below — a bench seat that lifts open for hidden storage, with visible cubby space underneath.
description:
Two storage zones live in this bench: a hinged-lid box on top for anything you'd rather keep out of sight, and an open cubby underneath, divided down the middle, for baskets or shoes you want within easy reach. Building it from a single sheet of plywood keeps material cost and waste low — plan your cuts before touching the saw, or have the sheet cut into two 4'x4' pieces at the store to make the individual cuts more manageable at home.

Get the lid overhang right: the plan's first pass at the lid width didn't leave any room to actually lift it, which is a mistake worth calling out because it's an easy one to repeat. Size the lid slightly wider than the box opening — a 1/4" to 1/2" overhang all around — so there's something to grip when you open it.

Check the box for square right after the back panel goes on, before the front and divider are permanently attached; it's your last easy chance to correct a racked box, and a bench that isn't square will bind against the hinged lid every time it closes.

materials.name: 3/4" plywood, 4x8 sheet
materials.note: Plan is sized for plywood that measures a true 3/4".

cutList.note: Slightly oversize the box opening (about 1/4"–1/2" overhang) so there's something to grip when lifting.

steps.title: Cut the plywood
steps.body: Cut all the plywood pieces from the sheet according to the cut list. Getting the sheet cut into two smaller pieces at the store first makes the individual cuts more manageable if you're working without a table saw or track saw.

steps.title: Assemble the sides
steps.body: Assemble each side panel with its 2x2 leg blocking using glue and 2" nails.

steps.title: Add the side trim
steps.body: Add the 1x3 trim to the top and bottom of each side panel, secured with glue and 1-1/4" finish nails.

steps.title: Attach the back
steps.body: Once both sides are complete, attach the back panel with glue and 2" nails, keeping the top edges flush. Check the assembly for square now, while it's still easy to correct — a racked box will bind against the lid later.

steps.title: Add the shelf and divider
steps.body: Install the bottom shelf panel, then the center divider that splits the lower cubby in two.

steps.title: Attach the front
steps.body: Add the front panel. Don't forget to nail up through the underside of the shelf into the front panel, not just through the front into the sides.

steps.title: Add the lid trim
steps.body: Cut and attach the 1x3 lid edge trim, measuring to fit rather than trusting the cut list exactly.

steps.title: Cut and hinge the lid
steps.body: Cut the lid panel with enough overhang beyond the box opening on all sides to actually get a grip when lifting it — about 1/4" to 1/2" is enough. Attach the lid to the back edge with your chosen hinge hardware.

images.alt: A storage bench with a hinged flip-top lid and an open divided cubby underneath
